course_info.title: Game Design Capstone Project
course_info.meeting_schedule: Monday, Wednesday, Friday - 4 hours per week

learning_objectives: Understand the capstone project scope, milestones, and evaluation criteria
Form balanced teams based on complementary skill sets
Begin ideation process using structured brainstorming techniques
readings: Fullerton, Game Design Workshop, Ch. 14 - Team Dynamics
Postmortem collection from Gamasutra (3 selected postmortems provided on LMS)

lecture_notes.introduction: The capstone project is your opportunity to demonstrate everything you have learned across the Game Design program. Over 15 weeks, your team will take a game from concept to gold build, producing a portfolio-quality project and a professional postmortem.
key_concepts: Milestone-driven development: concept, prototype, alpha, beta, gold
Team roles and skill assessment: identifying strengths and gaps
Scope management: fitting ambition to timeline and team capability
Risk identification and mitigation planning
Production tools and version control setup
lecture_notes.demonstration: Review of three previous capstone projects: one that succeeded by scoping well, one that pivoted mid-production, and one that overscoped and the lessons learned from each
setup_requirements: Git repository created for team (GitHub or GitLab)
Project management tool configured (Trello, Jira, or Notion)
Team communication channel established (Discord or Slack)

review.value: A narrow but fully polished cross-section of the game that demonstrates core mechanics, art quality, and technical capability at target fidelity
Quality, scope, and schedule form a constraint triangle; when one changes, at least one other must also change. The capstone fixes schedule, so scope must flex to maintain quality.

learning_objectives: Present a complete concept document covering vision, mechanics, audience, and scope
Receive and incorporate faculty and peer feedback on project feasibility
Establish a production plan with identified risks and contingencies
readings: Sample concept documents from industry (provided on LMS)
Adams, Fundamentals of Game Design, Ch. 4 - Concept Documents

lecture_notes.introduction: The concept document is your project's foundation. A strong concept document prevents costly direction changes later by forcing clarity on vision, scope, and feasibility before any production begins.
key_concepts: Concept document components: vision statement, core mechanics, target audience, platform, art direction, technical requirements
Elevator pitch: communicating the game's identity in 30 seconds
Competitive analysis: positioning your game within the existing landscape
Feasibility assessment: mapping required work against team skills and time
Risk register: identifying what could go wrong and how to respond
lecture_notes.demonstration: Each team presents their concept document (10 minutes per team) followed by structured faculty and peer critique using a standardized feedback form
setup_requirements: Completed concept document uploaded to LMS before class
Presentation prepared (slides or interactive prototype mockup)
Feedback forms printed for peer review

review.value: A concise description of the player experience that guides all design decisions: what the player does, how it feels, and why it matters
A living document listing identified risks, their likelihood, impact, and mitigation strategies, reviewed and updated at every milestone

learning_objectives: Demonstrate a playable prototype that validates the core game mechanic
Present playtesting data collected from external testers
Articulate a revised production plan based on prototype learnings
readings: Schell, The Art of Game Design, Ch. 23 - Playtesting
Fullerton, Game Design Workshop, Ch. 9 - Playtesting

lecture_notes.introduction: The prototype milestone is the most important checkpoint. It answers the fundamental question: is the core mechanic fun? If the prototype does not work, now is the time to pivot, not during alpha.
key_concepts: Prototype scope: core mechanic only, no polish, no secondary systems
Playtesting methodology: observation, think-aloud, post-session interview
Data collection: quantitative metrics (completion rates, time-on-task) and qualitative feedback
Pivot decision framework: when to iterate vs. when to change direction
Production plan revision based on prototype velocity data
lecture_notes.demonstration: Each team runs a live playtest session with classmates from other teams, collecting structured feedback using observation sheets and post-play questionnaires
setup_requirements: Playable prototype build uploaded and tested on target platform
Playtesting observation sheets and questionnaires prepared
Updated production timeline reflecting actual development velocity

review.value: The smallest possible implementation that allows testing whether the core interaction is engaging, stripped of all polish and secondary features
If playtesting reveals the core mechanic is not engaging, pivoting to a new mechanic is cheaper at prototype than iterating on something fundamentally flawed

learning_objectives: Deliver a feature-complete alpha build with all core systems functional
Present a bug tracking system with prioritized known issues
Demonstrate content pipeline and asset integration processes
readings: Chandler, Game Production Handbook, Ch. 12 - Alpha Milestone
Selected GDC postmortems on alpha-to-gold transitions (provided on LMS)

lecture_notes.introduction: Alpha means feature-complete: every intended system is implemented and functional, even if rough. After alpha, the focus shifts entirely to content completion, polish, and bug fixing. No new features after alpha.
key_concepts: Alpha definition: all features implemented, all content pipelines proven, major systems integrated
Feature lock discipline: resisting the urge to add features after alpha
Bug triage methodology: critical, major, minor, cosmetic classification
Content pipeline validation: ensuring all remaining assets can be produced and integrated on schedule
Performance profiling and optimization targets
lecture_notes.demonstration: Each team presents their alpha build with a structured walkthrough: feature inventory, known issue list, remaining content schedule, and risk assessment for the beta milestone
setup_requirements: Alpha build uploaded and tested on target platform
Bug tracking system (GitHub Issues, Trello, or similar) populated with known issues
Updated production schedule for beta milestone

review.value: The discipline of refusing all new feature requests after alpha, redirecting creative energy to polish, bug fixing, and content completion
Systematic classification of bugs by severity (crash, gameplay-breaking, visual, cosmetic) and priority (must-fix, should-fix, won't-fix) to focus limited time on the highest-impact issues

learning_objectives: Deliver a content-complete beta build ready for final polish
Present external playtesting results and balance adjustments
Demonstrate quality assurance processes and remaining bug fix plan
readings: Chandler, Game Production Handbook, Ch. 13 - Beta and QA
IGDA QA SIG best practices document (provided on LMS)

lecture_notes.introduction: Beta means content-complete: every level, asset, sound, and UI element is in the build. The remaining three weeks are dedicated entirely to polish, bug fixing, and preparing the gold build and postmortem.
key_concepts: Beta definition: content-complete, all assets integrated, in polish and QA phase
External playtesting at beta: fresh eyes on the complete experience
Balance tuning methodology: difficulty curves, economy balance, pacing
QA test plans: systematic coverage of all features and edge cases
Polish priorities: what to spend the final weeks on for maximum impact
lecture_notes.demonstration: Extended external playtest session with invited guests (students from other programs), followed by structured debrief and polish priority planning
setup_requirements: Beta build uploaded and tested on target platform
Test plan document covering all features and known edge cases
External playtest scheduled with at least 5 testers outside the team

review.value: The deliberate allocation of the final production weeks to the improvements that most affect player perception: first impressions, core loop feel, and ending experience
Re-testing previously fixed bugs and working features after each change to ensure bug fixes do not introduce new problems

class_schedule.topic: Milestone 5: Gold Build Presentation and Postmortem
learning_objectives: Deliver a final gold build suitable for public distribution
Present a professional postmortem analyzing the development process
Reflect on team dynamics, technical decisions, and personal growth
readings: Selected classic postmortems from Game Developer Magazine (provided on LMS)
Kreimeier, 'The 4+1 View Model of Game Design' (architecture reflection)

lecture_notes.introduction: The gold build is your finished game. The postmortem is your reflection on how you built it. Together, they represent the culmination of your game design education and become cornerstone portfolio pieces.
key_concepts: Gold master criteria: no critical or major bugs, all content final, performance targets met
Postmortem format: what went right (5 items), what went wrong (5 items), lessons learned
Honest self-assessment: acknowledging failures is more valuable than claiming perfection
Portfolio presentation: framing the project for future employers
Team retrospective: individual growth and collaborative lessons
lecture_notes.demonstration: Each team presents their gold build (15-minute live demo) followed by their postmortem presentation (10 minutes) with faculty Q&A. External industry guests provide feedback.
setup_requirements: Gold build uploaded to distribution platform (itch.io or similar)
Postmortem document completed and submitted (8-10 pages)
Presentation materials prepared for public showcase

review.value: The most valuable postmortems are brutally honest about failures and what caused them; hiring managers value self-awareness over claims of flawless execution
When presenting the capstone in your portfolio, clearly state your specific role and contributions, the challenges you personally overcame, and what you would do differently

major_assignments.title: Milestone 1: Concept Document
major_assignments.description: Produce a comprehensive concept document that defines the game's vision, core mechanics, target audience, art direction, technical requirements, production plan, and risk register. The document must be detailed enough for a faculty review board to assess project feasibility.
objectives: Define a clear and compelling game vision with an identifiable target audience
Document core mechanics with enough specificity to guide prototype development
Produce a realistic production plan with risk identification and mitigation strategies
Demonstrate collaborative team planning through role assignments and communication protocols
deliverables: Concept document (10-15 pages) covering vision, mechanics, audience, art direction, technical plan, and risk register
10-minute team presentation with visual aids
Production timeline with milestone dates and individual responsibility assignments
Team charter documenting roles, communication protocols, and conflict resolution process

criteria: Vision statement is clear, specific, and inspiring
Core mechanics are well-defined and feasible within the timeline
Target audience and platform are identified with supporting rationale
Art direction is coherent and achievable with team skills
Timeline is realistic with appropriate buffer for iteration
Risk register identifies at least 5 genuine risks with mitigation plans
Team roles leverage individual strengths appropriately
Scope is calibrated to team size and available time
Presentation communicates the game concept effectively in 10 minutes
All team members contribute to the presentation
Visual aids support rather than distract from the message
Team responds thoughtfully to faculty and peer questions
Comprehensive concept document demonstrating thorough pre-production planning
Realistic scope and production plan calibrated to team capabilities
Professional presentation communicating the project vision effectively
Strong team charter with clear roles and communication protocols

major_assignments.title: Milestone 2: Playable Prototype
major_assignments.description: Deliver a playable prototype that demonstrates the core game mechanic is functional and engaging. Include playtesting data from at least 8 external testers and a revised production plan reflecting actual development velocity.
objectives: Validate the core mechanic through a functional, playable prototype
Collect and analyze playtesting data to inform design iteration
Revise the production plan based on actual development velocity
Demonstrate effective use of version control and team collaboration tools
deliverables: Playable prototype build on target platform
Playtesting report with data from at least 8 external testers
Revised production plan with updated velocity estimates and milestone adjustments
Version control history showing consistent team commits and branching strategy

criteria: Core mechanic is implemented and playable without critical bugs
The prototype answers the fundamental question: is this fun?
Technical foundation supports the planned feature set
Build runs on target platform without errors
At least 8 external testers with documented sessions
Data collection includes both quantitative metrics and qualitative feedback
Analysis identifies clear patterns and actionable insights
Design changes are justified by testing data
Revised plan reflects honest assessment of team velocity
Scope adjustments are justified and documented
Version control shows consistent, meaningful commits from all team members
Team communication and task management tools are actively used
Functional prototype demonstrating an engaging core mechanic
Rigorous playtesting with data-driven design iteration
Honest production plan revision based on actual velocity
Strong team collaboration evidenced by version control and tools

major_assignments.title: Milestone 3: Alpha Build
major_assignments.description: Deliver a feature-complete alpha build with all core systems implemented and integrated. Include a comprehensive bug tracking system, performance profiling results, and content pipeline documentation proving all remaining assets can be completed on schedule.
objectives: Achieve feature completeness with all planned systems implemented
Establish a systematic bug tracking and triage process
Profile performance and identify optimization targets
Validate the content pipeline for remaining asset production
deliverables: Feature-complete alpha build on target platform
Bug tracking system with all known issues categorized by severity and priority
Performance profiling report with optimization targets and plan
Content pipeline documentation with remaining asset schedule

criteria: All planned features are implemented and functional
Systems are integrated and working together
No placeholder functionality remains in core systems
Feature lock is clearly established
Bug tracking system is populated and actively maintained
Bugs are properly classified by severity and priority
Critical and major bugs have assigned owners and target fix dates
Test coverage addresses all major features and interaction paths
Performance profiling identifies specific bottlenecks with data
Content pipeline is proven and remaining work is scheduled
Risk register is updated with current assessment
Beta milestone plan is realistic given remaining time
Feature-complete build demonstrating full system integration
Systematic QA process with comprehensive bug tracking
Data-driven performance assessment with clear optimization plan
Realistic beta plan proving project is on track for completion

major_assignments.title: Milestone 4: Beta Build
major_assignments.description: Deliver a content-complete beta build with all assets integrated, balance tuning based on external playtesting, and a comprehensive QA test plan. The build should be representative of the final game quality.
objectives: Achieve content completeness with all assets, levels, and UI elements integrated
Conduct balance tuning informed by external playtesting data
Execute a systematic QA test plan covering all features and edge cases
Prepare a realistic polish plan for the final three weeks
deliverables: Content-complete beta build on target platform
External playtesting report with at least 10 testers and balance analysis
QA test plan with execution results and remaining bug list
Polish priority plan for the gold milestone

criteria: All levels, assets, and UI elements are in the build
Audio and visual polish is near-final quality
No placeholder content remains in the player experience
Game is playable from start to finish
External playtesting conducted with at least 10 testers
Difficulty curve and pacing are informed by player data
Balance changes are documented with supporting evidence
Player feedback is addressed or intentionally deferred with rationale
Test plan covers all features, edge cases, and platform-specific issues
Test execution results are documented with pass/fail status
Remaining bugs are triaged with a realistic fix plan
Regression testing process is established for the final phase
Content-complete build demonstrating near-final quality
Balance tuning informed by rigorous external playtesting
Comprehensive QA coverage with systematic test execution
Clear polish plan prioritizing highest-impact improvements

major_assignments.title: Milestone 5: Gold Build and Postmortem
major_assignments.description: Deliver the final gold build of the game suitable for public distribution, accompanied by a professional postmortem document and public presentation. The gold build must meet all quality standards with no critical bugs. The postmortem must provide honest, detailed analysis of the development process.
objectives: Deliver a polished, complete game meeting gold master quality standards
Write a professional postmortem analyzing the full development process
Present the game and postmortem to faculty and industry guests
Reflect individually on personal growth and contributions
deliverables: Gold build uploaded to distribution platform (itch.io or equivalent)
Postmortem document (8-10 pages) with 5 things that went right, 5 things that went wrong, and lessons learned
Public presentation (25 minutes: 15-minute live demo + 10-minute postmortem)
Individual reflection essay (2-3 pages) on personal contributions and growth
Complete source code repository with documentation

criteria: No critical or major bugs in the release build
Game is playable from beginning to end without crashes
Visual and audio polish meets professional indie standards
Performance meets target framerate on intended platform
The game delivers on the vision established in the concept document
Honest and specific analysis of what went right and wrong
Root causes identified rather than surface-level observations
Lessons learned are actionable and demonstrate genuine reflection
Writing is professional and suitable for industry publication
Live demo runs smoothly and showcases the game effectively
Postmortem presentation is engaging and informative
All team members contribute meaningfully to the presentation
Q&A responses demonstrate deep understanding of the project
Honest assessment of personal contributions and growth areas
Specific examples of challenges overcome and skills developed
Connection between capstone experience and career goals
Self-awareness about strengths and areas for improvement
Polished gold build meeting professional indie quality standards
Insightful postmortem demonstrating genuine developmental learning
Professional public presentation to faculty and industry guests
Thoughtful individual reflection connecting experience to career growth

milestone_deliverables.description: Five milestone submissions tracking project progress

final_game.description: Quality and completeness of the final playable build

postmortem.description: Written analysis of development process and lessons learned

peer_evaluation.description: Team contribution assessment from all team members

course_policies.attendance: Attendance is mandatory for all milestone reviews, playtesting sessions, and the final presentation. Missing a milestone review without prior arrangement results in a zero for that milestone.
course_policies.late_submissions: Milestone deadlines are firm. Late milestone submissions are penalized 15% per day because subsequent milestones depend on timely delivery. The gold build deadline is absolute with no extensions.
course_policies.collaboration: This is a required team project. All team members must contribute substantively to every milestone. Peer evaluations are used to adjust individual grades. Free-riding will result in grade reduction.
course_policies.academic_integrity: All game code, assets, and documentation must be original team work or properly licensed third-party assets with attribution. Using unlicensed assets or submitting work from other courses will result in course failure.
course_policies.technology_requirements: Teams must maintain a shared version control repository (Git), project management tool, and communication platform. All builds must run on the target platform specified in the concept document.
